purpose: Every architectural side effect: filesystem mutation, database mutation, network request, subprocess execution, external API, scheduling, media generation, upload/download — with its initiating owner, authorization boundary, rollback capability, failure behaviour and observability.
supersedes: ../side_effects.json (Cycle 3). Cycle 5 RE-DERIVED every count by AST and reproduces Cycle 3 exactly on subprocess (32), Ledger.transaction (74), mkdtemp (1) and rmtree (2). ONE correction: see network_call_sites.

provenance.method: AST census over 127 modules (c5_deps.py) + Cycle-3 manual tracing of the publish/reconcile/gate/recovery paths.
provenance.self_correction: An earlier Cycle-5 pass reported 39 network sites. That was WRONG — the query matched `s.get(...)` on plain dicts. Filtering to literal `requests.<verb>` yields 15. This is the Cycle-2 method note biting again: A CENSUS IS ONLY AS GOOD AS ITS QUERY. Recorded rather than quietly fixed.

CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.status: DERIVED_FROM_CODE — regenerated by `python -m tools.arch regen`, enforced by rule ARCH-008. MIGRATION TARGET: point at derived/side_effects.json.
CYCLE_7_RECONCILIATION.mkdtemp_sites: *** 1 -> 2, AND THIS IS A FINDING, NOT A COUNT. ***

AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.the_claim: AR-11 / RC-10 / kb/risks.json state that compress.maybe_shrink_for_cap is 'the ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops (AST-confirmed)'. That claim was CERTAIN at fcffa73 and is FALSE at HEAD.
AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.the_second_mkdtemp: src/fanops/cli.py:1116 — `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='fanops_reframe_')` in cmd_reframe, added by #634. There is NO rmtree, NO finally, and NO TemporaryDirectory context manager. *** The scratch directory is created, written into, and never removed. ***
AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.verdict: *** RC-10 RECURRING — a SECOND instance of the exact defect class the audit named ('a mkdtemp with no owner'), introduced in the commit AFTER the contract froze. ***
AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.severity: LOW — and materially lower than the original. It is OPERATOR-INVOKED (`fanops reframe --dry-run`), leaks into the OS temp dir rather than into the project's own 04_agent_io, and the operator can override the location with --scratch. The original leaks once per oversize upload, FOREVER, into a persistent project directory (41 dirs / 924 MB accumulated, LS-5).
AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.why_it_matters_anyway: *** S09's scope is 'the SHRINK temp dir gets an owner'. It does NOT cover this one. An engineer who ships S09 and believes 'the mkdtemp leak is fixed' would be WRONG. *** Either widen S09, or file it separately — but do not let the audit's own claim of 'the ONLY mkdtemp' carry that belief.
AR-11_IS_NOW_FALSE_AS_WRITTEN.how_it_was_found: Rule ARCH-008 (every side effect is registered) fired on the FIRST run of the Cycle-7 governance system. Nobody read cli.py to find it.

network_call_sites.idempotency_key: *** NONE — the backend has none. This is why needs_reconcile exists as a distinct state. ***
network_call_sites.rollback: IMPOSSIBLE — park in needs_reconcile, NEVER re-POST

network_call_sites.why: the object key is fanops/{sha256(file)[:32]}{suffix} — CONTENT-ADDRESSED, so a re-upload overwrites the same object
NOT content-addressed — a retry creates a SECOND media object. An orphaned-media leak (R-03), NOT a double-publish.

network_call_sites.role: POSTIZ STATUS (+/-35d window — Postiz has NO per-post status endpoint)

network_call_sites.unknown_id_behaviour: *** RAISES RuntimeError on a 404 *** — the asymmetry is in the REMOTE API SHAPE, not in FanOps code (both clients raise identically on >=300). Root of C3-F2.

network_call_sites.note: *** NEW IN CYCLE 5 — Cycle 3's list of 15 omitted this site and counted meta_graph's INJECTABLE get instead. Both are real; the honest total is 15 literal requests.* sites PLUS meta_graph's injectable seam. ***
an INJECTABLE `get` (so tests never touch the network) — NOT a literal requests.* call, which is why an AST pass does not see it. The SOLE IG metric reader.

network_invariant: *** EVERY network call is OUTSIDE the ledger lock. The two publish POSTs are the only ones that mutate the outside world. ***

clip.py.what: ffmpeg render / crop / concat / subtitle burn

llm.py.what: *** claude -p — THE LLM IS A SUBPROCESS, NOT AN HTTP API. *** It rides the operator's existing `claude login`; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is NOT required, and `claude --bare -p` provably FAILS because it never reads the keychain.

post/compress.py.lock: *** NONE — runs in the LOCK-FREE NETWORK phase of _publish_one ***

postiz_lifecycle.py.what: *** docker — SHELLED FROM publish_due when `due` is non-empty (run.py:447-449). A subprocess side effect INSIDE the publish path. ***

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.op: BEGIN IMMEDIATE; guard state is queued AND due; post.state = submitting

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.note: *** F11 — the claim is persisted BEFORE any network I/O ***

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.defect: RC-10 mkdtemp leak; C4-F5 media_urls persisted into it

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.hazard: *** the throttle dict is per-PROCESS. N concurrent publisher processes publish N x the rate limit. ***

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.kind: *** THE EXTERNAL EFFECT. NOT IDEMPOTENT. NO KEY. NOT ROLLBACK-ABLE. ***

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.op: BEGIN IMMEDIATE; merge ONLY _NET_POST_FIELDS into a FRESHLY LOADED ledger

the_publish_side_effect_ordering.note: OUTSIDE the txn, fail-open — a full disk must NEVER roll back a committed publish

crash_critical_window: T2 -> T8. A crash anywhere in it leaves `submitting`, which publish_due never re-drives. THAT IS CORRECT. The defect is that reconcile cannot always terminate it.

the_network_ambiguity_decision_table.note: This table is the system's best engineering. It is correct on both backends.

5xx.why: the request landed; the response failed

2xx with no recognisable id.why: it may be live; we cannot address it

401.why: every post will fail; never burn the queue (INV-11)

the_network_ambiguity_decision_table.the_load_bearing_consequence: poster.publish NEVER lets a transient escape. The ONLY exception that escapes is AuthError, re-raised first. THEREFORE _publish_one's outer retry loop can only ever re-run the media UPLOAD — never the publish POST. This asymmetry is the ENTIRE double-publish defence and it is CORRECT.

authorization_boundaries.no_auto_publish: A post is BORN awaiting_approval; publish_due iterates `queued` ONLY; only Ledger.approve_post (guarded on awaiting_approval) promotes. INV-08 UPHELD. *** NOTHING publishes without an explicit operator approval, even on a live backend with the daemon running. ***
authorization_boundaries.go_live: golive.go_live is the ONLY setter of FANOPS_LIVE=1 (INV-18). Gated: accounts-valid -> >=1 live-ready channel -> past-due-backlog gate -> explicit confirm.
authorization_boundaries.irreversible_retire: *** THE ONE ASYMMETRY: adjust.retire (IRREVERSIBLE) is gated ONLY on cfg.is_live_backend, while every REVERSIBLE bias actuator waits for learning_validated AND p4_unlocked. RC-7. ***
authorization_boundaries.studio_routes: *** 149 routes, 108 mutating, 0 authenticated, 0 CSRF-protected. *** The boundary is the NETWORK INTERFACE (app.run(host=...), default 127.0.0.1:8787). A RECORDED, ACCEPTED decision (studio/CLAUDE.md), not an oversight — but it IS the only control.

sites.note: SECRETS go to the OS KEYRING ONLY. The ONLY writer of FANOPS_LIVE (INV-18).

propagation.daemon: re-reads .env (override=True) AND rebuilds Config EVERY TICK — a go_live reaches it within one tick
propagation.studio: load_dotenv ONCE at process entry, then blocks in app.run — a .env change made by the CLI or daemon NEVER reaches a RUNNING Studio (COUP-02b)
propagation.config: properties are os.getenv PER ACCESS (uncached), so a write is visible to the WRITING process immediately

failure_semantics.house_norm: fail-open with a LOGGED breadcrumb. Enforced in CI by tests/test_swallow_ratchet.py, which FAILS a NEW silent broad `except`.
failure_semantics.the_one_fail_CLOSED_dependency: *** [framing]/cv2. With smart_framing ON (the DEFAULT) and cv2 absent, framing.require_cv2 raises ToolchainMissingError -> EXIT 2. Every other optional extra fails OPEN. INV-17. ***

failures_that_look_like_SUCCESS_to_the_c.lie: an UNRECOGNISED backend returns a DryRunPoster ON A LIVE SYSTEM without raising. The live-guard is case-INSENSITIVE on the literal 'dryrun'; the PROVIDERS lookup is case-SENSITIVE. They disagree.
returns the SUCCESS-SHAPED string 'submitting' after doing NOTHING. The post vanishes from publish_due's tally entirely.
returns a NON-ZERO count while nothing persisted. Blast radius NIL today — the caller discards the value.
returns the PARTIALLY accumulated face list — a crash after 3 of 5 faces is indistinguishable from 'there were 3 faces'.

surfaces: heartbeat (emitted ONLY when a pass COMPLETES)
Studio Home banner (the ONLY surface that probes the backend live)

misleading_success_surfaces.lie: reports `alive` while EVERY pass halts. daemon_progress treats 'the newest run.log line of ANY kind' as liveness, and a failing pass still writes stage lines. heartbeat_age_s IS in the dict; the VERDICT discards it.
SUPPRESSED whenever ONE valid channel remains — so the single-malformed-channel case (the MORE likely one) shows a healthy system while one channel silently publishes nothing.

genuinely_silent: the fanops-shrink-* disk leak (no log, no metric, no doctor check)
a torn attempts.json resetting the gate ceiling
intro_match gate accumulation (doubly invisible — gate_source_id returns None, so pipeline_status OMITS it)
observability.the_best_failure_surface: the `gave_up` digest bucket — which NEVER FIRES ON ZERNIO (C3-OBS-7).
